channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: S&P 500 futures tick higher as oil falls and traders await Nvidia’s earnings: Live updates
item.description: Chipmaker and AI darling Nvidia will report first-quarter earnings after Wednesday's closing bell.

item.title: Iran threatens to extend conflict ‘beyond the region’ if U.S. and Israel resume attacks
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard on Wednesday threatened to extend the Middle East conflict "beyond the region" if the U.S. and Israel resume attacks against Tehran.

item.title: Target beats Wall Street estimates, hikes sales outlook as shoppers start to return
item.description: Target has been struggling with a sales slump and decreased customer traffic, though CEO Michael Fiddelke has said the retailer is poised for a turnaround.

item.title: Stellantis CEO Antonio Filosa is about to unveil his plan to turn the company around as the automaker's stock lags
item.description: Shares of Stellantis are off nearly 30% since Antonio Filosa, a veteran who climbed through the company ranks, was named CEO nearly a year ago.

item.title: AllianceBernstein reveals its top defense stock pick as Europe's military manufacturers retreat
item.description: Budget uncertainty and "procurement bureaucracy" have hindered Europe's defense sector this year, AllianceBernstein said.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Lowe's, Cava, Target & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Taiwan was front and center during Trump's China trip — but absent from Xi’s meeting with Putin
item.description: The meetings between China's Xi Jinping and his U.S. and Russian counterparts came just days apart, but there was a stark contrast in tone and content.

item.title: Lowe's beats Wall Street expectations against 'challenging' housing backdrop
item.description: Lowe's beat Wall Street expectations for its fiscal first-quarter earnings and reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

item.title: Trump's past tax returns get protection from IRS enforcement under 'lawfare' fund settlement
item.description: President Trump agreed to drop his lawsuit against the IRS in exchange for a fund that can compensate allies who were targeted under the Biden administration.

item.title: Rising mortgage rates cause surge in demand for riskier loans
item.description: Mortgage rates continued to rise last week, causing more borrowers to head to adjustable-rate loans, which are cheaper but considered riskier.

item.title: More car buyers are shifting to EVs — but the reasons why are nuanced
item.description: Trade in data from Edmunds shows more car buyers are choosing EVs, as fuel prices spike. But other factors might be holding back demand.

item.title: College career path ‘over’ as skilled trade get 30% pay bump, recruitment giant says
item.description: "I would say you can make a good career and good money in skilled trade. That's definitely a career track," CEO of world's largest recruitment firm told CNBC.

item.title: Democrats make demands of U.S. trade representative ahead of U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement review
item.description: CNBC obtained a letter from a group of Senate Democrats to U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer laying out what they want to see in a key trade agreement.

item.title: SpaceX picks Goldman Sachs for lead left position on record-breaking IPO, sources say
item.description: SpaceX has picked Goldman Sachs to lead what's expected to be a record-setting IPO.

item.title: U.S. expected to unveil criminal charges against Cuba's Raul Castro
item.description: The charges against Raul Castro are expected to be based on a 1996 incident in which Cuban jets shot down planes operated by a group of Cuban exiles.

item.title: Vance defends stock trading spree in Trump financial filings: 'Come on, man'
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said at the White House on Tuesday that he and President Donald Trump both support banning congressional stock trading.

item.title: Breakthrough agreement in housing bill gives investors wins
item.description: The U.S. House is set to approve an updated housing affordability bill after removing industry-opposed requirements on selling build-to-rent homes.

item.title: This trucking stock is poised to stage a comeback, Jefferies says
item.description: C.H. Robinson Worldwide has underperformed its peers recently, but the stock is bound to bounce due to its ongoing strategy shift, per Jefferies.

item.title: Senate advances measure to end military action in Iran in rebuke to Trump
item.description: The war with Iran has now blown past the 60-day requirement under the War Powers Act for the president to seek the authorization of Congress.

item.title: Home Depot was a mistake but selling it now would compound it. Why we're hanging on
item.description: Home Depot worked its way modestly higher after a down open on a quarterly update that was just OK and a status quo outlook.

item.title: A strange paradox has taken hold of the global fashion industry
item.description: Fashion brands are doubling down on sustainability even as value-seeking shoppers ignore the pitch.

item.title: See if you qualify for Bank of America's $2.25 million ATM fee settlement
item.description: The bank was accused of double-charging customers for balance inquiries. Plus: How to avoid out-of-network ATM fees.

item.title: Alibaba reveals more powerful Zhenwu AI chip, new LLM
item.description: Alibaba announced updates to its AI offerings, including a more powerful chip and a new large language model.

item.title: Google debuts new AI models, personal AI agents in effort to keep pace with OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Google made a number of AI announcements at its annual developer conference, rolling out more-advanced models and agentic tools for its expansive user base.

item.title: EU clears major hurdle to finalize U.S. trade pact — and sidestep Trump tariff hikes
item.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on Wednesday welcomed a provisional agreement on legislation to remove import duties on U.S. goods.

item.title: China confirms order for 200 Boeing planes, calls aviation key area for U.S. cooperation
item.description: China has agreed to buy 200 Boeing jets, its first major order in nearly a decade, U.S. President Donald Trump said last week.

item.title: Over 47,000 Samsung Electronics workers set to strike as wage talks break down, sending shares lower
item.description: More than 47,000 Samsung Electronics workers will begin a strike on Thursday after wage negotiations between the company and its union broke down.

item.title: OpenAI announces new Guaranteed Capacity offering for customers to secure compute
item.description: Altman said OpenAI will make sure it leaves enough capacity available for its products like ChatGPT and its coding assistant Codex.

item.title: Polymarket launches private company trading so investors can speculate on Anthropic, OpenAI
item.description: The company is launching prediction markets tied to private company milestones, including valuations, IPO timing and secondary-market activity for names like OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Pentagon taps Shield AI for low-cost drone program as Iran war accelerates demand for cheap drones
item.description: Shield AI is fresh off a $1 billion funding round, which valued the company at $12.7 billion
